title: Pulsars
script_summary: An exploration of pulsars that uncovers their unique characteristics and significance in the universe.

segments.voiceover: Science has a clearer story about pulsars than social media does.
Pulsars are highly magnetized, rotating neutron stars, emitting beams of radiation.
They spin rapidly—up to 700 times a second—resulting in a lighthouse effect.
Discovered in 1967, these cosmic clocks help measure time and test gravity.
But uncertainty remains—what happens when pulsars die?
Want to dive deeper into space mysteries? Hit follow and explore together!

full_voiceover: Science has a clearer story about pulsars than social media does. Pulsars are highly magnetized, rotating neutron stars, emitting beams of radiation. They spin rapidly—up to 700 times a second—resulting in a lighthouse effect. Discovered in 1967, these cosmic clocks help measure time and test gravity. But uncertainty remains—what happens when pulsars die? Want to dive deeper into space mysteries? Hit follow and explore together!
